# cortexcloud_appsec_policy (Resource)

Manages an Application Security policy.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# AppSec policy for critical findings
resource "cortexcloud_appsec_policy" "critical_findings" {
  name        = "Critical Findings on Production"
  description = "Alert on critical security issues"
  status      = "enabled"

  # Conditions as JSON (supports up to 10 levels of nesting)
  conditions = jsonencode({
    AND = [
      {
        SEARCH_FIELD = "Severity"
        SEARCH_TYPE  = "EQ"
        SEARCH_VALUE = "CRITICAL"
      }
    ]
  })

  # Each trigger block must be present on CREATE/UPDATE — the API rejects
  # requests that omit any of periodic / pr / cicd / ci_image / image_registry
  # with HTTP 422 ValidateError.
  periodic_trigger = {
    enabled = true
    actions = {
      report_issue = true
    }
  }

  pr_trigger = {
    enabled = true
    actions = {
      report_issue      = true
      report_pr_comment = true
      block_pr          = false
    }
  }

  cicd_trigger = {
    enabled = false
    actions = {
      report_issue = false
      block_cicd   = false
      report_cicd  = false
    }
  }

  ci_image_trigger = {
    enabled = false
    actions = {
      report_issue = false
      report_cicd  = false
      block_cicd   = false
    }
  }

  image_registry_trigger = {
    enabled = false
    actions = {
      report_issue = false
    }
  }

  asset_group_ids = [1]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `conditions` (String) Policy conditions as JSON-encoded string with nested AND/OR logic.
- `name` (String) Name of the policy.

### Optional

- `asset_group_ids` (List of Number) List of asset groups to which the policy applies. If the array is empty, the policy applies to all asset groups. Mutually exclusive with scope.
- `ci_image_trigger` (Attributes) CI image scan trigger configuration. Defaults to disabled if this attribute is not configured. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--ci_image_trigger))
- `cicd_trigger` (Attributes) If true, the policy is evaluated on CI/CD pipeline events. Defaults to disabled if this attribute is not configured. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--cicd_trigger))
- `description` (String) Description of the policy.
- `image_registry_trigger` (Attributes) Image registry scan trigger configuration. Defaults to disabled if this attribute is not configured. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--image_registry_trigger))
- `override_issue_severity` (String) Override severity for issues.
- `periodic_trigger` (Attributes) If true, the policy is evaluated periodically (for example, daily or weekly). Defaults to disabled if this attribute is not configured. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--periodic_trigger))
- `pr_trigger` (Attributes) If true, the policy is evaluated on Pull Request (PR) events. Defaults to disabled if this attribute is not configured. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--pr_trigger))
- `scope` (String) Asset targeting scope as JSON-encoded string. Mutually exclusive with asset_group_ids.
- `status` (String) Indicates whether the policy is enabled or disabled.

### Read-Only

- `created_by` (String) The user or system that created the policy.
- `date_created` (String) The date and time when the policy was created.
- `date_modified` (String) The date and time when the policy was last modified.
- `developer_suppression_affects` (Boolean) Whether developer suppression affects the policy. This field is read-only as the API does not honor this value on create or update.
- `id` (String) Unique identifier for the policy.
- `is_custom` (Boolean) Indicates if the policy is a custom policy or a system-provided policy.
- `modified_by` (String) The user or system that last modified the policy.
- `version` (Number) The version of the policy - goes up by one every policy update.

<a id="nestedatt--ci_image_trigger"></a>
### Nested Schema for `ci_image_trigger`

Required:

- `actions` (Attributes) Actions to take when the policy detects its target risk and the policy is triggered. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--ci_image_trigger--actions))
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the CI image trigger is enabled.

Optional:

- `override_issue_severity` (String) Set the severity of the issue (and override the system severity). If not used, system severity is kept.

<a id="nestedatt--ci_image_trigger--actions"></a>
### Nested Schema for `ci_image_trigger.actions`

Required:

- `block_cicd` (Boolean) Indicates if triggering the policy should block the CI/CD pipeline.
- `report_cicd` (Boolean) Indicates if triggering the policy should soft fail the CI/CD pipeline in the platform.
- `report_issue` (Boolean) Indicates if triggering the policy should create an issue in the connected system.



<a id="nestedatt--cicd_trigger"></a>
### Nested Schema for `cicd_trigger`

Required:

- `actions` (Attributes) Actions to take when the policy detects its target risk and the policy is triggered. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--cicd_trigger--actions))
- `enabled` (Boolean) Enables the CI/CD trigger for the policy.

Optional:

- `override_issue_severity` (String) Set the severity of the issue (and override the system severity). If not used, system severity is kept.

<a id="nestedatt--cicd_trigger--actions"></a>
### Nested Schema for `cicd_trigger.actions`

Required:

- `block_cicd` (Boolean) Indicates if triggering the policy should block the CI/CD pipeline. Ensure that the CI/CD trigger is selected.
- `report_cicd` (Boolean) Indicates if triggering the policy should soft fail the CI/CD pipeline in the platform. Ensure that the CI/CD trigger is selected.
- `report_issue` (Boolean) Indicates if triggering the policy should create an issue in the connected system.



<a id="nestedatt--image_registry_trigger"></a>
### Nested Schema for `image_registry_trigger`

Required:

- `actions` (Attributes) Actions to take when the policy detects its target risk and the policy is triggered. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--image_registry_trigger--actions))
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the image registry trigger is enabled.

Optional:

- `override_issue_severity` (String) Set the severity of the issue (and override the system severity). If not used, system severity is kept.

<a id="nestedatt--image_registry_trigger--actions"></a>
### Nested Schema for `image_registry_trigger.actions`

Required:

- `report_issue` (Boolean) Indicates if triggering the policy should create an issue in the connected system.



<a id="nestedatt--periodic_trigger"></a>
### Nested Schema for `periodic_trigger`

Required:

- `actions` (Attributes) Actions to take when the policy detects its target risk and the policy is triggered. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--periodic_trigger--actions))
- `enabled` (Boolean) Enables the periodic trigger for the policy.

Optional:

- `override_issue_severity` (String) Sets the severity of the issue, overriding the system severity. If not configured, the system severity is used.

<a id="nestedatt--periodic_trigger--actions"></a>
### Nested Schema for `periodic_trigger.actions`

Required:

- `report_issue` (Boolean) Indicates if triggering the policy should create an issue in the connected system.



<a id="nestedatt--pr_trigger"></a>
### Nested Schema for `pr_trigger`

Required:

- `actions` (Attributes) Actions to take when the policy detects its target risk and the policy is triggered. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--pr_trigger--actions))
- `enabled` (Boolean) Enables the PR trigger for the policy.

Optional:

- `override_issue_severity` (String) Set the severity of the issue (and override the system severity). If not used, system severity is kept.

<a id="nestedatt--pr_trigger--actions"></a>
### Nested Schema for `pr_trigger.actions`

Required:

- `block_pr` (Boolean) Indicates if triggering the policy should block the pull request. Ensure that the PR trigger is selected.
- `report_issue` (Boolean) Indicates if triggering the policy should create an issue in the connected system.
- `report_pr_comment` (Boolean) Indicates if triggering the policy should create comments on the pull request. Ensure that the PR trigger is selected.
